title: Multiplexed scNOMe-seq Protocol Based on Isolated Single Nuclei
author: Sebastian Pott
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.btutnnwn
materials: Denature the samples by placing the 384-well PCR plates on a thermocycler and run the following program: 95°C 3 minutes

Prior to denaturing the samples, make Adaptase Master Mix and keep sealed on ice: A B C 1 Reagent Volumes for 1 96-well plate (+10 extra reactions) (µL) Volumes for single reaction (µL) 2 Elution Buffer (Qiagen) 450.5 4.25 3 Buffer G1 212 2 4 Reagent G2 212 2 5 Reagent G3 132.5 1.25 6 Enzyme G4 53 0.5 7 Enzyme G5 53 0.5 8 Total 1113 10.5 Reaction volume: 20.5 µL
After FACS, heat plates to 50ºC for 20 minutes in a thermocycler to facilitate nuclei lysis. 50 °C, 20 minutes Store plates at -20ºC or move on to the next step.
Denature the samples by placing 96-well plates on a thermocycler and run the following program: 95°C 3 min

steps.title: Before commencing with nuclei isolation and...
steps.instruction: Before commencing with nuclei isolation and GpC Methyltransferase step, prepare 384- or 96-well collection plates with digestion mix . This can be prepared the day before and kept at 4ºC. %s %s

steps.title: To prepare Proteinase K , add 1
steps.instruction: To prepare Proteinase K , add 1.04 mL of Proteinase K Storage Buffer to one tube of Proteinase K (as per Zymo Kit instructions).

steps.title: Also prepare a large quanity of...
steps.instruction: Also prepare a large quanity of RSB buffer (10X ) before beginning nuclei isolation and treatment. From that, make a 1:10 dilution (i.e., RSB buffer 1X) for use in the experiment. Methylation-sensitive single-molecule analysis of chromatin structure. Current protocols in molecular biology.

steps.title: RSB buffer (10X) recipe
steps.instruction: RSB buffer (10X) recipe: 100mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.4 100mM NaCl 30mM MgCl 2 This stock can be stored for up to one year at 4ºC.

steps.title: Mix enough RSB buffer (1X) for...
steps.instruction: Mix enough RSB buffer (1X) for the rest of the nuclei isolation and GpC methylation step (make 5 mL to be safe).

steps.title: Start with a suspension of single cells
steps.instruction: Start with a suspension of single cells. Count cells and use ~5-10M cells for this protocol.

steps.title: Transfer cells to 15 mL Falcon tube
steps.instruction: Transfer cells to 15 mL Falcon tube.

steps.title: Spin for 5 minutes at 500xg at 4°C
steps.instruction: Spin for 5 minutes at 500xg at 4°C. 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard supernatant and wash once with ice cold PBS
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant and wash once with ice cold PBS. Spin for 5 minutes at 500xg at 4°C. 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard supernatant and resuspend cells in...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant and resuspend cells in 1 mL ice-cold RSB buffer (1X).

steps.title: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00, at room temperature

steps.title: Add 15 µL 1% NP-40 solution (0
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL 1% NP-40 solution (0.015% final concentration) to the cell suspension. 15 µL, NP-40 (1%) The NP-40 concentration might need to be adjusted depending on cell type

steps.title: Transfer cell suspension to a dounce...
steps.instruction: Transfer cell suspension to a dounce tissue grinder (2 mL volume) and burst the cells with 15 strokes of the pestle (both A and B work).

steps.title: Transfer lysed cells to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer lysed cells to a 1.5 mL eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells for 5 minutes at 800xg at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells for 5 minutes at 800xg at 4°C. 800 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Wash with 1 mL 1X RSB buffer (without NP-40). 1 mL, 1X RSB (without NP-40)

steps.title: Incubate in RSB buffer for 30...
steps.instruction: Incubate in RSB buffer for 30 seconds – 1 minute 00:00:30 - 00:01:00

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 800xg at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 800xg at 4°C. 800 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard supernatant and resuspend in 1X...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant and resuspend in 1X GpC methyltransferase buffer such that there are 1M cells per 75 µL. If there are <1M cells, resuspend the pellet in 75 µL

steps.title: Prepare a 1
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1.5 mL eppendorf tube with GpC Methylase Reaction Mix for incubation: A B C 1 Reagent Reaction concentration (based on reaction volume) Amount (µL) 2 GpC methyltransferase buffer (10X) (NEB) 0.5X 7.5 3 SAM (32mM) (NEB) 320µM 1.5 4 GpC Methytransferase (4U/µL) (NEB) 1.33U/µL 50 5 Distilled H2O 16 6 Nuclei 75 7 total 150 Reaction volume: 150 µL After adding 75 µL of nuclei to the final mixtures of the above ingredients, pipette to mix.

steps.title: Incubate at 37ºC for 7
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37ºC for 7.5 minutes. 37 °C, 7.5 minutes

steps.title: Add a boost of 25 µL...
steps.instruction: Add a boost of 25 µL GpC Methyltransferase (100U) and 0.75 µL 32mM SAM to the nuclei. 25 µL, GpC methyltransferase 0.75 µL, 32mM SAM

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 7
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 7.5 minutes. 37 °C, 7.5 minutes

steps.title: Add 500 µL 1X PBS and...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL 1X PBS and spin for 5 minutes at 800 g at 4°C. 500 µL, of 1X PBS 800 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 0
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 0.5-1 mL 1X PBS. 500 µL – 1 mL, 1X PBS

steps.title: Add 2 drops of Hoechst
steps.instruction: Add 2 drops of Hoechst. If the cells are resuspended in <500 µL of 1X PBS, use 1 drop of Hoechst

steps.title: Keep on ice for ~15 minutes before FACS sorting
steps.instruction: Keep on ice for ~15 minutes before FACS sorting. On ice, ~15 minutes

steps.title: Add 15 µL CT conversion reagent...
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL CT conversion reagent to each well of 2 384-well plate. Pipette up and down 8 times to mix the sample. 15 µL, CT conversion reagent

steps.title: To prepare CT Conversion Reagent add...
steps.instruction: To prepare CT Conversion Reagent add the following buffers to one bottle of CT Conversion Reagent: 7.9 mL M-Solubilization Buffer 3 mL M-Dilution Buffer Once the reagent is fully dissolved through shaking and vortexing vigorously, add: 1.6 mL M-Reaction Buffer

steps.title: Seal the plates with adhesive film...
steps.instruction: Seal the plates with adhesive film and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature. 2000 x g, Room temperature, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place the plate in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a thermocycler and run the following program: 98°C 8 minutes 64°C 3.5hrs 4°C Hold

steps.title: Prior to cleaning up bisulfite conversion...
steps.instruction: Prior to cleaning up bisulfite conversion reactions, make Random Primer Solution for each of the 8 primers being used such that each is at a final molarity of 500nM. Keep on ice. %s %s

steps.title: Place two Zymo-Spin 384-Well DNA Binding Plate on two 2
steps.instruction: Place two Zymo-Spin 384-Well DNA Binding Plate on two 2.0 mL 96-Well Deep Well Plates.

steps.title: Load 80 µL M-Binding Buffer to...
steps.instruction: Load 80 µL M-Binding Buffer to each well of the Zymo-Spin 384-Well DNA Binding Plates. 80 µL, M-Binding Buffer

steps.title: Transfer bisulfite conversion reactions to the...
steps.instruction: Transfer bisulfite conversion reactions to the Zymo-Spin 384-Well DNA Binding Plates. Pipette up and down 8 times to mix the sample.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg. 5000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard the flow through by decanting...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through by decanting and add 100 µL M-Wash Buffer to each well of the 384-Well DNA Binding Plates. 100 µL, of M-Wash buffer

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg. 5000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard the flow through by decanting...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through by decanting and add 50 µL M-Desulphonation Buffer to each well of the 384-Well DNA Binding Plates. 50 µL, M-Desulphonation Buffer

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 15 minutes. Room temperature, 15 minutes

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg. 5000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard the flow through by decanting...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through by decanting and add 100 µL M-Wash Buffer to each well of the 384-Well DNA Binding Plates. 100 µL, M-Wash Buffer

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg. 5000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Repeat wash steps (39 and 40) once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash steps (39 and 40) once more.

steps.title: Place the 384-Well DNA Binding Plates...
steps.instruction: Place the 384-Well DNA Binding Plates on 2 new 384-well PCR plates. Add 7 µL Random Primer Solution to each well of the 384-Well DNA Binding Plates (fig. 1). 7 µL, Random Primer Solution Figure 1a. Primer scheme for plate 1 (the 8 wells inside of the red squares will be pooled into a single well of a 96-well plate in the sample cleanup step). Figure 1b. Primer scheme for plate 2 (the 8 wells inside of the red squares will be pooled into a single well of a 96-well plate in the sample cleanup step).

steps.title: Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 5,000xg and discard the 384-Well DNA Binding Plate. 5000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Seal the 384-well PCR plate with...
steps.instruction: Seal the 384-well PCR plate with adhesive film and store at -20°C for up to 1 week. -20 °C, for up to 1 week

steps.title: Prior to denaturing the samples, make...
steps.instruction: Prior to denaturing the samples, make Random Priming Master Mix and keep sealed on ice: A B C D 1 Reagent Reaction concentration (based on reaction volume) Volumes for 2 384-well plates (+ 20%) (µL) Volumes for single reaction (µL) 2 Blue Buffer (10X) 1X 922 1 3 Klenow exo (50U/µL) 1.25U/µL 231 0.25 4 dNTP (10mM each) 500uM each 461 0.5 5 Distilled H2O 2995 3.25 6 Total 4609 5 Reaction volume: 10 µL Reaction volume is 10 µL because it's assumed that 2 µL is lost during the centrifugation in step 44 (Luo et al., 2018)

steps.title: Immediately place the plate on ice for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Immediately place the plate on ice for 2 minutes. On ice, 2 minutes

steps.title: Add 5 µL Random Priming Master...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL Random Priming Master Mix to each well of the 384-well PCR plates. 5 µL, Random Priming Master Mix

steps.title: Vortex to mix the samples and...
steps.instruction: Vortex to mix the samples and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg. 2000 x g, °C, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place the plates in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plates in a thermocycler and run the following program: 4°C 5 minutes 25°C 5 minutes 37°C 60 minutes 4°C Hold

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µL Exo/rSAP Master Mix to each well of the 384-well PCR plates. 1.5 µL, Exo/rSAP Mix A B C D 1 Reagent Reaction concentration (based on reaction volume) Volumes for 2 384-well plates (+20%) (µL) Volumes for single reaction (µL) 2 Exonuclease I (20U/µL) 1.74U/µL 922 1 3 rSAP (1U/µL) 0.043U/µL 461 0.5 4 Total 1383 1.5 Reaction volume: 11.5 µL

steps.title: Vortex and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg
steps.instruction: Vortex and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg. 2000 x g, °C, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place the plate in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a thermocycler and run the following program: 37°C 30 min 4°C Hold

steps.title: Before proceeding with sample clean-up, prepare...
steps.instruction: Before proceeding with sample clean-up, prepare enough SPRI beads (14 mL) for the whole experiment. Store beads at 4°C and always take out 30 minutes prior to use.

steps.title: Mix Sera-Mag SpeedBeads and transfer 280 µL to a 1
steps.instruction: Mix Sera-Mag SpeedBeads and transfer 280 µL to a 1.5 mL tube. 280 µL, Sera-Mag SpeedBeads

steps.title: Place beads on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place beads on a magnetic stand until the solution is clear of beads. Carefully remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash twice with 1 mL TE
steps.instruction: Wash twice with 1 mL TE. For each wash, remove the tube from the magnet and mix by inversion. 1 mL, TE

steps.title: Resuspend washed beads in 280 µL TE
steps.instruction: Resuspend washed beads in 280 µL TE. 280 µL, TE

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.52 g PEG 8000 to a new 50 mL sterile conical tube. 2.52 g, PEG 8000

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.8 mL of 5M NaCl to the 50 mL tube. 2.8 mL, 5M NaCl

steps.title: Add 140 µL 1M Tris-HCl pH=8
steps.instruction: Add 140 µL 1M Tris-HCl pH=8.0 and 28 µL of 0.5M EDTA pH=8.0 to the 50 mL tube. 140 µL, 1M Tris-HCl pH=8.0 28 µL, 0.5M EDTA pH=8.0

steps.title: Add 7-8 mL distilled H 2...
steps.instruction: Add 7-8 mL distilled H 2 O and mix until PEG 8000 dissolves into solution. 7 mL, distilled H2O – 8 mL, distilled H2O

steps.title: Add the washed Sera-Mag SpeedBeads to...
steps.instruction: Add the washed Sera-Mag SpeedBeads to the 50 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Add enough distilled H 2 O...
steps.instruction: Add enough distilled H 2 O to bring the total volume up to 14 mL. Vortex before each use.

steps.title: Add 73
steps.instruction: Add 73.6 µL (0.8x) SPRI beads to each well of a clean 96-well PCR plate. 73.6 µL, SPRI beads

steps.title: Pool the samples from the two...
steps.instruction: Pool the samples from the two 384-well PCR plates to one 96-well PCR plate such that each well of the 96-well PCR plate holds a pool of 8 samples, with each of those samples having been indexed with a different distinct random primer during step 42. (two rows of each 384-well plate combine in one row of the 96-well plate.)

steps.title: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. Room temperature, for 5 minutes

steps.title: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature
steps.instruction: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature. 2000 x g, Room temperature, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place the 96-well PCR plate on...
steps.instruction: Place the 96-well PCR plate on the DynaMag TM -96 Side Magnet and let stand until the solution in each well is clear of beads (~5 minutes).

steps.title: Wash beads 3 times with 150 µL fresh 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Wash beads 3 times with 150 µL fresh 80% EtOH. 150 µL, 80% EtOH

steps.title: Remove all EtOH and let beads dry at room temperature
steps.instruction: Remove all EtOH and let beads dry at room temperature. Do not overdry the beads.

steps.title: Remove the plate from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the magnet and add 10 µL Elution Buffer (Qiagen) to each well. Resuspend beads by pipette. 10 µL, Elution Buffer (Qiagen)

steps.title: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. Room temperature, for 5 minutes

steps.title: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature
steps.instruction: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature. 2000 x g, Room temperature, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place back on magnet and let...
steps.instruction: Place back on magnet and let stand until solution is clear (~5 minutes).

steps.title: Transfer 10 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 10 µL of the supernatant from each well to a clean 96-well PCR plate. The plate can be stored at -20ºC. 10 µL, of supernatant

steps.title: Immediately place the plate on ice for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Immediately place the plate on ice for 2 minutes. On ice, 2 minutes

steps.title: Place the plate in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a thermocycler and run the following program: 37°C 30 min 95°C 2 min 4°C Hold

steps.title: Add 5 µL PCR Primer Mix to every well
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL PCR Primer Mix to every well. 5 µL, PCR Primer Mix

steps.title: Dilute each P5L primer such that...
steps.instruction: Dilute each P5L primer such that the final concentration is 600nM (0.6µM) after the two primers corresponding to each well are combined %s %s

steps.title: Dilute each P7L primer such that...
steps.instruction: Dilute each P7L primer such that the final concentration is 1µM after the two primers corresponding to each well are combined. %s %s

steps.title: To a new 96-well PCR plate,...
steps.instruction: To a new 96-well PCR plate, add 3 µL of each P5L primer to individual columns and 3 µL of each P7L primer to individual rows (one P5L primer per column, one P7L primer per row). Each well contains the PCR Primer Mix for the corresponding well in the sample plate. (Fig. 2) Figure 2. Each well receives a unique P5L-P7L primer combination.

steps.title: Transfer 5 µL of the appropriate...
steps.instruction: Transfer 5 µL of the appropriate primer mix to the wells of the sample plate. 5 µL, PCR Primer Mix

steps.title: Add 25 µL 2X KAPA HiFi Mix to each well
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL 2X KAPA HiFi Mix to each well. 25 µL, 2X KAPA HiFi Mix

steps.title: Vortex and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg
steps.instruction: Vortex and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg. 2000 x g, °C, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place the plate in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a thermocycler and run the following program: a. 95°C 2 minutes b. 98°C 30 seconds c. 98°C 15 seconds d. 64°C 30 seconds e. 72°C 2 minutes Return to step c 14 times (15 cycles total) f. 72°C 5 minutes g. 4°C Hold Number of cycles can be adjusted

steps.title: Add 40 µL (0
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL (0.8x) SPRI Beads to each well of the 96-well PCR plate containing the sample. 40 µL, SPRI beads

steps.title: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. Room temperature, for 5 minutes

steps.title: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature
steps.instruction: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature. 2000 x g, Room temperature, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place 96-well plates on DynaMag™-96 Side...
steps.instruction: Place 96-well plates on DynaMag™-96 Side Magnet, let stand until solution in wells is clear of beads (~5 minutes).

steps.title: Remove supernatant and wash beads 2...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and wash beads 2 times with 150 µL freshly made 80% EtOH. 150 µL, 80% EtOH

steps.title: Remove all EtOH after the last...
steps.instruction: Remove all EtOH after the last wash and remove plate from magnet. Let beads dry at room temperature. DO NOT overdry beads.

steps.title: Add 25 µL Elution Buffer (Qiagen)...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL Elution Buffer (Qiagen) and resuspend beads by pipette. 25 µL, Elution Buffer (Qiagen)

steps.title: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Vortex and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. Room temperature, for 5 minutes

steps.title: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature
steps.instruction: Quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg at room temperature. 2000 x g, Room temperature, 10 seconds

steps.title: Place back on magnet and let...
steps.instruction: Place back on magnet and let stand until solution is clear of beads (~5 minutes).

steps.title: Combine 25 µL eluent from all...
steps.instruction: Combine 25 µL eluent from all wells in each column of the 96-well plate (8 wells per column, 12 columns) into 12 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. 25 µL, eluent

steps.title: Add 160 µL (0
steps.instruction: Add 160 µL (0.8x) SPRI Beads to each 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Pipette to mix and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. 160 µL, SPRI Beads

steps.title: Place 1
steps.instruction: Place 1.5 mL tubes on DynaMag™-2 Magnet, let stand until solution in tubes in clear of beads (~5 minutes).

steps.title: Remove supernatant and wash beads 2...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and wash beads 2 times with 500 µL fresh 80% EtOH. 500 µL, 80% EtOH

steps.title: After the last wash, remove all...
steps.instruction: After the last wash, remove all EtOH and let beads dry at room temperature. DO NOT overdry beads.

steps.title: Add 40 µL Elution Buffer (Qiagen)...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL Elution Buffer (Qiagen) and resuspend beads by pipet. Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. 40 µL, Elution Buffer (Qiagen)

steps.title: Place tubes back on magnet and...
steps.instruction: Place tubes back on magnet and let stand until solution is clear of beads (~5 minutes).

steps.title: Transfer 40 µL supernatant to 12 new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 40 µL supernatant to 12 new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. 40 µL, eluent

steps.title: Measure concentration of each 1
steps.instruction: Measure concentration of each 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube with Qubit dsDNA BR Assay Kit.

steps.title: Add 10
steps.instruction: Add 10.5 µL Adaptase Master Mix to each well of the 96-well PCR plate. 10.5 µL, Adaptase Master Mix

steps.title: Vortex and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg
steps.instruction: Vortex and quick spin for 10 seconds at 2,000xg. 2000 x g, °C, 10 seconds
